linkedin.text:
The question every HR or marketing leader should ask their event partner: what's your client return rate?

Anyone can show you a highlight reel. Fewer can show you clients who came back — and then came back again.

At Uproduction Events, 1,500+ delivered events and 25,000+ participants aren't vanity metrics. They're the compounded result of conference agendas that held attention, incentive trips that changed how teams felt about their company, and production execution that made senior leadership look good.

If you're evaluating partners for a conference, convention, or incentive trip — the right conversation starts with their track record, not their pitch deck.

What's the one thing you always check when vetting a new event production partner? Drop it in the comments.

instagram.text:
1,500+ events. 25,000+ participants. 130+ destinations worldwide.

The stat that means the most to us? The clients who keep coming back.

Repeat business is the only social proof that can't be faked. And after 16 years in corporate event production, it's the number we're proudest of.

Drop a 🌍 in the comments if you're planning an international incentive or corporate event — we'll reach out.

#IncentiveTravel #EventProduction #MICE

facebook.text:
When someone asks us how we know our events actually work — we point to the numbers.

1,500+ events delivered. 25,000+ participants across 130+ destinations. Clients who came back for their third, fourth, fifth event with us.

That kind of repeat business doesn't happen by accident. It happens when the conference runs on time, the incentive trip genuinely moves people, and the gala dinner becomes a story employees tell for years.

Planning something for your team this year? Tell us what you're building — we'd love to think through it with you.

image_prompt: A wide-angle photograph of 400 corporate guests in elegant evening attire filling a grand open-air terrace overlooking a lit Mediterranean coastline, candid conversations and laughter captured mid-moment, warm golden lighting, cinematic depth.
